meta.description: Download our free contractor invoice template. Perfect for independent contractors to bill for labor, materials, and track project milestones. Get paid faster.

blocks.title: Contractor Invoice Template
blocks.subtitle: A professional invoice template designed specifically for independent contractors. Easily bill for labor, separate material costs, and look professional.

blocks.authorBio: Jane is a certified CPA with over 15 years of experience in small business accounting.

blocks.heading: Why Contractors Need specialized Invoices
blocks.text: Contracting work often involves a mix of hourly labor, flat-rate project fees, and reimbursable material expenses. A standard retail invoice doesn't provide the necessary structure to clearly itemize these different cost centers. Using a dedicated contractor invoice reduces client confusion, minimizes payment disputes, and ensures you aren't left out-of-pocket for project materials.

summaryItems: Always separate labor costs from material costs.
Include reference numbers to any signed Statements of Work (SOW) or initial estimates.
Ensure your payment terms align with your contractor agreement (e.g., Net 15 or Net 30).
Attach receipts for any reimbursable expenses if billing at cost.

features.title: Material & Labor Splits
features.description: Clearly define what the client is paying for to prevent scope disputes and justify your total project cost.

features.title: Milestone Billing Ready
features.description: Perfect for billing 30% upfront, 30% midway, and 40% upon completion.

features.title: Mobile Friendly
features.description: Generate an invoice right from the job site on your phone and email the PDF instantly.

blocks.title: Contractor Invoice vs. Standard Invoice
rows: May have complex labor vs material tax rates

steps.title: Add Your DBA and License Info
steps.description: Add your contractor business name and, if applicable in your state/region, your contractor license number to establish trust.

steps.title: Reference the Original Estimate
steps.description: Add a note linking this invoice back to the original approved estimate or contract number. This speeds up approval from the client.

steps.title: Itemize Labor and Materials
steps.description: Do not lump everything together. List 'Drywall Materials - $500' separate from 'Installation Labor - 10 Hours at $75/hr'.

steps.title: State Your Payment Terms
steps.description: Clearly state when the payment is due (e.g., Net 30) and list all accepted payment methods (Bank Transfer, Check, etc.).

mistakes.solution: If you mark up materials, it's often best practice to agree on the markup percentage in the initial contract rather than hiding it in a lump-sum invoice.

mistakes.mistake: Forgetting to invoice for change orders.
mistakes.solution: If the client requested additional work mid-project, list those specific 'Change Orders' as separate line items so they understand why the total differs from the estimate.

questions.question: Can I use an invoice generator for contractor work?
questions.description: Yes. A professional invoice generator allows you to add custom line items for both hourly labor and flat-rate materials, making it perfect for contractors.

questions.question: Should contractors charge sales tax on labor?
questions.description: This depends entirely on your local state or country laws. In some jurisdictions, materials are taxable but labor is not. Always consult a local tax professional.

blocks.heading: Ready to bill your client?
blocks.subtext: Use our free invoice generator to create a professional contractor invoice in seconds.
